key_insight: A fracture-aware poroelastic model can be made locking-free and computationally cheaper by reformulating it with total pressure and solving flow before mechanics after the first time step.
engineering_relevance: This enables more stable and efficient simulation of coupled fluid–solid behavior in fractured porous media, which is important for reservoir engineering, geomechanics, and subsurface design.
method: Reformulate the mixed-dimensional Biot/fracture system into a four-field model: displacement, total pressure, matrix pressure, and fracture pressure.
Use a time-decoupled strategy: fully coupled solve at the initial step, then sequential flow-then-mechanics solves thereafter.
Add a stabilization term in the mechanics equation; discretize with Taylor–Hood elements for displacement/total pressure and Lagrange elements for pressures.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical energy stability, error estimates, and numerical experiments supporting the semi-discrete and fully discrete decoupled scheme.
watch_item: The stabilization introduces parameter-dependent assumptions, so practical robustness may depend on mesh choice and model coefficients.
paper_title: A time-decoupling scheme for mixed-dimensional poroelastic models with fractures
